other_observers: none; Rich Miller found it the day before.
specific_location: Below the dam at Lake DeWeese
description: A warbler shaped song bird, that was lemon yellow underneath, with dark feathers going under the eye down each side of the throat. The throat itself was bright yellow. The undertail coverts were also bright yellow. The area from above the bill going above the eye and around the eye was yellow on each side. The crown was grayish green/olive. The back and wings were the same color, of green/olive. The tail was the same color, of green/olive. The lower maniable of the bill was pinkish, the upper maniable of the bill was dark. The bill was pointed and thin. The legs were pink and then. The bird wasn't bright enough for a male I don't think.Behaviors: Seen in some bushes in the open, I wish I had the camera.Call: nonePlumage: adult
